data.body:
PASS 1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT APPLICABLE / PASS.

This Cluster 2 plan is a pure internal refactor of `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py` — collapsing `_compute_backoff_seconds` and `_cas_backoff_seconds` into one parameterized `_jittered_backoff(...)` helper. It integrates no external API/CLI/service/library and asserts no new capability. The only "integration" is `random.uniform` (stdlib, already used at lines 136 and 428) and the existing in-file `_rebase_retry`/`_concurrency` path — both internal and already in use. Per the T3 overlay's not-applicable clause, an internal, already-used integration routes to PASS.

Affirmations (criteria satisfied):
- The plan explicitly preserves behavior: "performs exactly one `random.uniform` call (preserving the RNG draw count/order, so a seeded sequence is identical)" — confirmed against existing code, which makes exactly one `random.uniform` call per backoff function.
- No new external surface, subcommand, or endpoint is named; AC4 explicitly forbids a new `_retry.py` module / universal `retry()`, keeping the change in-file.
- No auth/HTTPS preconditions apply (no network/service).

No T3 findings.
